title: trifolium andersonii var. beatleyae
intro: Trifolium andersonii var. beatleyae, also known as Beatley's bean or Beatley's clover, is a variety of Trifolium andersonii, a perennial legume native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to inhibit the growth of certain pathogens and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Trifolium andersonii var. beatleyae include its potential to support cardiovascular health, lower blood sugar levels, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
